title: Per-tool renderers — Tier 2, Tier 3, and verbosity toggle
purpose: Complete the tool renderer set with compact and minimal renderers for the remaining tools, and implement the Ctrl+O verbosity toggle that controls whether future tool calls render in collapsed or expanded mode.
task: Implement Tier 2 compact renderers in tools/compact.rs: Write, WebSearch, WebFetch, and one-line renderers for LSP, Task, Skill, RunScript, ToolSearch. Implement Tier 3 minimal renderers in tools/minimal.rs: SpawnAgent (creates a status line in the fixed panel), Fork, SignalAgent, WaitAgent (invisible), CloseAgent. Add the Ctrl+O verbosity toggle to the rendering state, affecting whether future tool calls show header-only (collapsed) or header+body (expanded). Register all renderers in the renderer_for factory from NT-006.

requirements.title: Write renderer
requirements.spec: The Write renderer SHALL show file path, line count, and AST status on one line.
acceptance: Header: '+ {path}' with line count and AST status (ok / error)
body() returns None (no expanded view)
Test: header_line for a write call includes path and line count

requirements.title: Web renderers
requirements.spec: WebSearch SHALL show query and result count. WebFetch SHALL show URL and content length. Both on one line.
acceptance: WebSearch header: 'web: {query}' with result count
WebFetch header: 'fetch: {url}' with content length
Both return None for body()
Test: WebSearch header includes query text

requirements.title: Remaining Tier 2 compact renderers
requirements.spec: LSP, Task, Skill, RunScript, and ToolSearch SHALL each render as a one-line summary.
acceptance: LSP: 'lsp: {action}' with target
Task: 'task: {action} "{title}"' with status
Skill: 'skill: {name} loaded'
RunScript: 'script: {name}' with output preview
ToolSearch: 'search tools: {query}' with match count
All return None for body()
Test: Task header includes action and title

requirements.title: SpawnAgent renderer — fixed panel status line
requirements.spec: WHEN SpawnAgent completes, THE SYSTEM SHALL create an agent status line in the fixed panel rather than rendering in the scroll region. A brief note in the scroll region confirms the spawn.
acceptance: SpawnAgent does NOT render a tool output block in the scroll region
Instead, it emits an AgentSpawned signal that NT-009's status line system consumes
A single-line note ('spawned: {agent_name}') renders in the scroll region for awareness
Test: SpawnAgent renderer returns a SpawnSignal action instead of scroll region content

requirements.title: WaitAgent — invisible rendering
requirements.spec: WaitAgent SHALL NOT render as a tool call. The wait is invisible to the user; the result appears when the awaited agent completes.
acceptance: renderer_for('wait_agent') returns a renderer whose header_line returns an empty string
The renderer signals to the rendering pipeline that no output should be written to the scroll region
Test: WaitAgent renderer produces no visible output

requirements.title: Remaining Tier 3 minimal renderers
requirements.spec: Fork, SignalAgent, and CloseAgent SHALL render as minimal one-line entries in the scroll region.
acceptance: Fork: 'fork → {model}' with task preview
SignalAgent: '→ {agent_path}: {message_preview}'
CloseAgent: '✕ {agent_path}' with cascade count
All return None for body()
Test: CloseAgent header includes cascade count

requirements.title: Verbosity toggle (Ctrl+O)
requirements.spec: WHEN the user presses Ctrl+O, THE SYSTEM SHALL toggle the global verbosity setting, affecting whether future tool calls render header-only (collapsed) or header+body (expanded). Content already in scrollback SHALL NOT be affected.
acceptance: VerbosityState enum: Collapsed (default), Expanded
Ctrl+O flips between Collapsed and Expanded
When Collapsed, tool calls render header_line only (body is not written to scroll region)
When Expanded, tool calls render header_line followed by body (if body() returns Some)
The toggle does not modify or rewrite any content already in the scroll region
A brief status message ('verbose: on' / 'verbose: off') appears in the streaming indicator area momentarily
Test: toggling from Collapsed to Expanded changes the state

boundaries: SHALL NOT modify the ToolRenderer trait or Tier 1 renderers — those are NT-006
SHALL NOT implement agent status line rendering — that is NT-009
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
verification: renderer_for returns correct renderer for all tool names — verified by test
WaitAgent produces no visible output — verified by test
SpawnAgent produces a spawn signal instead of scroll content — verified by test
Verbosity toggle changes state — verified by test
